Sandwich ELISA

Sample preparation
1. Collect blood samples in tubes with anti-coagulant.
2. Centrifuge the samples at 1,000-10,000 x g for 10 min at 4°C and remove the pellet.
3. Aliquot the supernatant into several tubes (minimum 50 µL each).

Capture antibody coating
4. Dilute the capture antibody in coating buffer to 1-10 µg/mL.
5. Add ~50 µL of diluted capture antibody to each well and cover the plate. Incubate with gentle agitation for 2 h at room temperature, or overnight at 4°C.
6. Wash each well three times with wash buffer.

Blocking and sample addition
7. Add 200 µL of blocking buffer to each well and cover the plate. Block with gentle agitation for 1-2 h at room temperature.
8. Dilute the samples, controls and standards in dilution buffer.
9. Add 100 µL of diluted samples and standards to the wells and cover the plate. Incubate with gentle agitation for 2 h at room temperature.
10. Wash each well three times with wash buffer.

Detection
11. Add 100 µL of pre-diluted detection antibody to each well and cover the plate. Incubate for 2 h at room temperature.
12. Wash each well three times with wash buffer.
13. Add 100 µL of HRP-conjugated secondary antibody to each well and incubate for 1-2 h at room temperature.
14. Wash each well three times with wash buffer.
15. Add 50-100 µL of TMB enzyme substrate to each well and incubate with gentle agitation on a plate shaker.
16. Add 100 µL of stop solution to each well and shake for 1 min to mix.
17. Read the absorbance in the plate reader at 450 nm.
